title: cornus stolonifera var. californica
intro: Cornus stolonifera var. californica, also known as Red-osier Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to western North America, characterized by its red stems and white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial in treating various health conditions, including wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bark and roots of the plant contain medicinal compounds that can help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Cornus stolonifera var. californica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, such as diarrhea, colds, and flu.
